title: forestiera pubescens subsp. neomexicana
intro: Forestiera pubescens subsp. neomexicana, also known as New Mexico boxelder or desert boxelder, is a shrub or small tree species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Forestiera pubescens subsp. neomexicana include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and prevent infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, and it can also be used in tincture or salve form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
